Project.Name: zenmoney-scripts
Project.Summary: Small read-only helper scripts for ZenMoney API (Scriptcraft: spec-first, stdlib-first, narrative logs).

Minimalism.Sufficiency: Build only what is needed for the stated PURPOSE and VERIFY scenarios. Avoid "nice-to-have" expansions.
Minimalism.StdlibFirst: Prefer native/stdlib solutions (especially in Python). Add third-party dependencies only with explicit justification.
Minimalism.NoAutoChoice: Do not auto-select language or tools. Propose 1-2 options with arguments; require user approval.

Observability.LogFormat: Logs must be narrative, line-oriented, stdout only. Use: Step N — ..., Info — key=value (optional), OK — ..., WARN — ..., ERROR — ..., HINT — ... Do NOT output logs as JSON. Final line must be either: OK — ... OR ERROR — ...
Observability.VerifyPolicy: Each script must define at least 2 VERIFY scenarios (happy path + failure/edge). Verification requires a real smoke run of those scenarios.

Integration.SecretsPolicy: Secrets must be provided via environment variables. Never print secrets in logs.

S-001.Name: zenmoney-balance
S-001.Purpose: Fetch current account balances from ZenMoney API (read-only) and print summary to stdout. Not in scope: creating/updating/deleting any entities; OAuth2 interactive flows; long-running sync.

Interface.Args: --account <id> (optional; filter to one account) archived accounts are INCLUDED by default --exclude-archived (exclude archived accounts) --in-balance-only (default: true) --all-accounts (set inBalance-only to false) --timeout-sec <n> (default: 20) --full (expanded output: account list + per-currency totals + total_base) --json (stdout JSON-only; logs go to stderr)
Interface.EnvVars: ZENMONEY_API_KEY (provided by user; used as Bearer token value)
Interface.Outputs:
default (no flags): narrative logs ending with: OK — balance fetched and includes: Info — total_base[BASE_CUR]=...
--full: one line per account: <title> [<CUR>] (<uuid>) <balance> (archived) # suffix only when archive=true plus totals per currency: Info — total[CUR]=... plus total converted into user's base currency (user.currency): Info — total_base[BASE_CUR]=... final line: OK — balance fetched
--json: stdout JSON-only (no narrative logs on stdout)

Language.Rationale: Both can be dependency-free. Python tends to be simplest for cron + small CLIs; Node is fine if you prefer JS ecosystem.

Pipeline.step-1: Validate args and required env var (ZENMONEY_API_KEY present; timeout numeric).
Pipeline.step-2: Call ZenMoney API Diff endpoint (POST https://api.zenmoney.ru/v8/diff/) with Authorization: Bearer <ZENMONEY_API_KEY>.
Pipeline.step-3: Extract accounts[*].balance and currency via account.instrument -> instrument.shortTitle, and instrument rates.
Pipeline.step-4: Determine base currency via user.currency -> Instrument.id.
Pipeline.step-6: Print per-account balances + totals per currency + total_base converted into base currency; exit 0.

Safety.Guards: Never log token. Treat HTTP 5xx/timeouts as exit 10 (retryable). Treat 401/403 as exit 20.

Scenario-2.Expected: OK — balance fetched; includes Info — total_base[BASE_CUR]=...; exit code 0

Scenario-3.Expected: stdout is JSON-only with ok=true and data.total_base + data.base_cur; exit code 0

S-002.Name: zenmoney-add-transaction
S-002.Purpose: Add a single income/expense transaction into ZenMoney via the Diff endpoint (POST /v8/diff/). Not in scope: editing/deleting existing transactions; transfers with different income/outcome accounts; merchant/suggest.

Interface.Args:
Required: --amount <number> --type income|expense --account <account-title-substring-or-uuid> --category <tag-title-substring-or-uuid>
Optional: --comment <text> --date <YYYY-MM-DD> (default: today) --timezone <IANA tz> (default: system) --timeout-sec <n> (default: 20)
Matching rules: - Account/tag can be provided as UUID; otherwise treated as a case-sensitive substring match. - Accounts with archive=true are ignored (not selectable).
Interface.Outputs: On success: prints a short confirmation line with type, amount, base fields (account/category/date) and the created transaction id. On failure: prints ERROR/WARN/HINT lines explaining what was not found / ambiguous.

Language.Rationale: Python is simplest for a small CLI with date parsing + HTTP + UUID without extra deps.

Pipeline.step-1: Validate args and env (ZENMONEY_API_KEY present; amount numeric; type allowed; date format ok).
Pipeline.step-2: Fetch current data via Diff first sync (POST /v8/diff/ with serverTimestamp=0).
Pipeline.step-3: Resolve account: match by UUID or title substring; reject archive=true accounts; handle 0 matches and >1 matches (ambiguous).
Pipeline.step-4: Resolve category/tag: match by UUID or title substring; handle 0 matches and >1 matches.
Pipeline.step-5: Derive instrument from account.instrument (use for incomeInstrument/outcomeInstrument).
Pipeline.step-6: Determine user id to set transaction.user (source: diff response user[0].id).
Pipeline.step-7: Build Transaction object (UUID id; created/changed timestamps; date; income/outcome based on type; tag list; comment).
Pipeline.step-8: Send transaction via Diff POST including transaction:[...].
Pipeline.step-9: Check response contains transaction; print confirmation; exit 0.

State.Dedup: none (each run creates a new UUID)

Safety.DangerousActions: Writes a new transaction into ZenMoney (irreversible via this script).
Safety.Guards: - Never log token. - If account/category resolution is ambiguous, exit 2 and print candidate titles. - If HTTP 5xx/timeout: exit 10. - If 401/403: exit 20.

Scenario-2.Expected: ERROR — account not found; exit code 2

S-003.Name: zenmoney-dict
S-003.Purpose: Read-only dictionary/reference printer for ZenMoney entities. It fetches a first-sync Diff snapshot (serverTimestamp=0) and prints selected entity lists (accounts, categories/tags, instruments, user base currency).

Interface.Args:
Optional filters: --accounts <substring> (filter by account.title; default: all) --tags <substring> (filter by tag.title; default: all) --instruments <substring> (filter by instrument.shortTitle/title; default: all)
Optional outputs: --no-accounts --no-tags --no-instruments --no-user
--timeout-sec <n> (default: 20)
Interface.Outputs:
stdout text: Accounts: title | id | archive | inBalance | instrument Tags: title | id | parent Instruments: shortTitle | id | rate User: id | currency (base instrument id)
Final line: OK — dict printed OR ERROR — ...

Language.Rationale: Python is simplest for a small CLI with filtering and stable text output.

Safety.Guards: Never log token. HTTP 5xx/timeouts -> exit 10. 401/403 -> exit 20.

Scenario-2.Expected: OK — dict printed; output includes matching account titles; exit code 0

S-004.Name: zenmoney-transfer
S-004.Purpose: Create an in-app transfer between two non-archived accounts by posting a single Transaction with both outcome and income equal to the transfer amount. Not in scope: currency conversion; multi-leg transfers; fees; editing/deleting transactions.

Interface.Args:
Required: --from <uuid|title-substring> --to <uuid|title-substring> --amount <number> (accepts forms like 1000, 1000.50, 1 000,50)
Optional: --comment <text> (default: "Transfer") --date <YYYY-MM-DD> (default: today) --timezone <IANA tz> (default: system) --timeout-sec <n> (default: 20)
Matching rules: - Accounts can be provided as UUID; otherwise treated as a case-sensitive substring match. - Accounts with archive=true are ignored (not selectable).
Interface.Outputs: On success: prints a short confirmation and ends with `OK — transfer added`. If account instruments differ: prints `WARN — instruments differ (mixed currency transfer 1:1)` and still proceeds.

Language.Rationale: Python is simplest for a small CLI with date parsing + HTTP + UUID without extra deps.

Pipeline.step-1: Validate args and env (ZENMONEY_API_KEY present; amount numeric after normalization; date format ok).
Pipeline.step-2: Fetch snapshot via Diff first sync (POST /v8/diff/ with serverTimestamp=0).
Pipeline.step-3: Resolve from/to accounts; reject archive=true; handle not found/ambiguous.
Pipeline.step-4: Compare instruments; if different, print WARN and proceed (1:1 transfer).
Pipeline.step-6: Build Transaction with income=amount, outcome=amount; outcomeAccount=from; incomeAccount=to; instruments set from source account instrument.
Pipeline.step-7: Send via Diff POST including transaction:[...].
Pipeline.step-8: Check response contains transaction; print confirmation; exit 0.

State.Dedup: none (each run creates a new UUID)

Safety.DangerousActions: Writes a new transfer transaction into ZenMoney.
Safety.Guards: - Never log token. - If account resolution is ambiguous, exit 2 and print candidate titles. - If HTTP 5xx/timeout: exit 10. - If 401/403: exit 20.

Scenario-1.Command: unset ZENMONEY_API_KEY; python3 scripts/zenmoney_transfer.py --from X --to Y --amount 1

Scenario-2.Expected: ERROR — from account not found; exit code 2
